manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: buffer, slack, glare, peep, rack, repair, swarm, stalk, peel.
Your opponent's team words are: scrub, paste, host, fry, spell, rock, pawn, tip.
Distractor words are: pool, pad, strip, shock, rush, rag, fold.
The kill words are: drone.

message.content:
that.CLUE: computing
TARGETS: buffer, slack, rack, swarm, repair
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, list them. Must not be any word from any list nor contain parts. Provide in format.

Our words: buffer, slack, glare, peep, rack, repair, swarm, stalk... peel.

We need to give clue that connects to multiple of them.

Potential clue: "eye"? Eye relates to glare, peep (peek maybe), stalk? maybe not. Buffer? Slack? Not.

Clue: "maintenance"? relates to repair, buffer? maybe.

Clue: "insect"? relates to swarm, stalk? maybe peep (as in peep of baby bird). Not.

Clue: "tape"? connects to peel (tape peel), slack (tape slack), buffer (tape buffer)? Not.

Clue: "spool"? Not allowed because distractor includes pool. But "spool" includes "pool" part? It contains "pool". That may be considered containing part? Probably not allowed.

Need one-word clue not from any list. Could be "adjust". Relates to slack, buffer, repair? Maybe.

Maybe choose "repair" etc. Actually we need hints for multiple.

Look at our words: buffer (like memory buffer), slack (like slack variable), glare (bright light), peep (look quickly), rack (display), repair (fix), swarm (group of insects), stalk (follow), peel (remove skin).

Potential common theme: "skin"? Peel relates, glare (skin?), not.

Common theme: "software"? buffer (computing), slack (messaging app), repair (patch), swarm (algorithm), rack (server rack), peep (ping? no). Not.

Perhaps "tool"? Could relate to repair, rack, peel (peeler), but also buffer (tool). Not.

Maybe "garden"? swarm (bees), stalk (plant stems), peel (fruit), rack (garden rack?), glare (sun), peep (chick), buffer (soil buffer). Too many.

Clue: "sun"? Relates to glare, peel (sun peel), swarm (mosquitoes). Maybe.

But we want multiple words; maybe 3-4.

Consider clue "insect": swarm (insects), stalk (like a stalk of a plant? but insects stalk prey), peel? Not.

Clue "film": buffer (film buffer), glare (film glare), peep (peep show), rack (film rack?), not.

Clue "repair": but you can't use target word itself as clue? It says clue cannot be one of the words in the lists. 'repair' is in our list, can't use.

Clue "rest": buffer (rest buffer?), slack (rest slack?), no.

Clue "light": glare (light), peep (peep of light), maybe.

Clue "support": buffer supports, rack supports, slack (support?), not.

Clue "tension": slack opposite tension, buffer reduces tension, rack? not.

Clue "cable": rack holds cables, slack cable, buffer? not.

Clue "garden": swarm (bees), stalk (plants), peel (fruit), not.

Maybe choose to clue three: "bees"? swarms of bees, peel (banana?), no.

Better: "repair", "buffer", "slack" all relate to "computer". Buffer (data), slack (communication platform), repair (debug), rack (server rack), swarm (distributed computing). So clue "computing". That relates to buffer, slack, rack, swarm, repair maybe. But "slack" is a brand name not general word, might be okay.

Check restriction: clue cannot contain parts of any word in lists. 'computing' doesn't contain any. Good.

Thus we can provide clue "computing" targeting buffer, slack, rack, swarm, repair maybe. But risk: opponent words like "host" also computer term. But okay.

We need to list which targets we intend. Provide maybe 5.

But more targets increase risk of opponent guess? Actually partner might guess all correctly; the opponent may also see clue and guess? But that's fine.

We'll list: buffer, slack, rack, swarm, repair.

Clue: "computing". So format:

CLUE: computing
TARGETS: buffer, slack, rack, swarm, repair

Check that 'computing' is not part of any list. Good.

Return as only
